text:
Alex Jones was found liable before damages were decided.

In the Sandy Hook cases, the courts entered default judgments against Jones and his companies because of repeated discovery violations and failure to comply with court orders. That meant legal responsibility for defamation and related claims was established by the judge before the damages trials.

The later jury trials were mainly about how much he had to pay, not whether he was liable in the first place.

So, to answer directly: juries did not first decide whether he was legally responsible. Liability had already been determined; the juries then determined damages.
